title: Outdoor Bench with Arbor
summary: A garden bench with a slatted overhead arbor on 4x4 posts — a shaded sitting spot, built for a permanent, anchored footing rather than a freestanding patio bench.
description:
This bench's arbor top is what separates it from an ordinary garden bench: two tall 4x4 posts per side support a slatted lattice roof, tall enough to sit under and sturdy enough to eventually host a climbing vine. Set the posts in concrete footings before building anything else — this is a genuinely top-heavy structure once the arbor slats are on, and it is not designed to be freestanding or moved once assembled.

Build the bench seat as its own low unit between the posts first, at a comfortable seat height, then add the tall back posts and arbor structure above it. The arbor's cross beams get through-bolted to the posts rather than merely screwed, since this joint carries the full cantilevered weight of the slatted roof and any wind load on it.

The roof slats space evenly across the top beams and can be left as an open lattice for partial shade, or built tighter for more coverage — that spacing is a genuine style choice with no structural consequence either way. **This is an anchored, permanent garden structure, not a portable bench** — verify local frost-depth requirements for the footings, and never skip the concrete footing step even if the ground looks firm.
timeLabel: 8–12 hrs across a weekend, plus footing cure time

materials.name: 2x4 pressure-treated, 8 ft
materials.note: Seat slats and arbor cross beams.

materials.name: Carriage bolts, 3/8" x 6"
materials.note: Arbor cross beam to post joints.

cutList.note: Length includes footing depth; verify against local frost-depth requirements before final cut.
Through-bolted to the posts, not screwed.

steps.title: Set the posts
steps.body: Dig four post holes to a depth that meets your local frost-depth requirement. Set the posts, brace plumb with a level, and pour concrete footings. Allow the concrete to cure fully before continuing.

steps.title: Build and attach the seat
steps.body: Build the seat frame, attach it between the posts at a comfortable seat height with 3" exterior deck screws, then attach the seat slats across the frame.

steps.title: Attach the arbor cross beams
steps.body: Drill through both posts and each cross beam, and secure with 3/8" x 6" carriage bolts — this joint carries the full weight and wind load of the roof, so do not substitute screws.

steps.title: Add the roof slats
steps.body: Space and attach the arbor roof slats evenly across the cross beams with 2-1/2" exterior deck screws.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces, then seal or stain the entire structure with an exterior-rated finish.

unresolvedImages.alt: Garden bench with a slatted overhead arbor on posts
